moat.title: The Compliance
moat.subtitle: Direct mapping of SupraWall capabilities to EU AI Act requirements.

art9.req: Prove your AI had a documented risk management system before it acted
art9.sol: Policy Engine: pre-execution allow/deny rules. Compliance Templates: one-click Article 9 policy activation for Banking, Healthcare, HR.

art12.req: Log every AI action in an auditable, tamper-proof record
art12.sol: RSA-signed audit trail at the tool-call boundary. Every execution logged with cryptographic signature.

art14.req: A human must be able to monitor and intervene in AI actions
art14.sol: Human approval workflows for flagged tool calls. Policy engine is human-defined; AI operates within human-set boundaries.

art9.title: Article 09: Risk Management
art9.desc: Evidence-based risk assessments for autonomous agent workflows.

art12.title: Article 12: Auditable Logging
art12.desc: Tamper-proof, cryptographically signed record of every agentic intervention.

art14.title: Article 14: Human Oversight
art14.desc: Enforcing manual approval gates for sensitive agentic outcomes.

conformity.description: SupraWall turns regular agentic workflows into "High-Risk Compliant" architectures with zero friction. From Article 9 to 14, we've handled the engineering so you can handle the product.

final.description: Don't build a compliance team. Enable SupraWall Audit Trail and get back to building the product.

hero.title: One Import.
hero.description: Deterministic safety templates for every high-risk AI category. Each template pre-configures the 13 mandatory baseline controls plus sector-specific logic for EU AI Act Annex III.

grid.title: Sector-Specific Templates
grid.subtitle: Select your industry to see the regulatory requires and technical configuration.

buildVsBuy.title: Build vs. Template
buildVsBuy.subtitle: The true cost of implementing EU AI Act compliance from scratch.

biometrics.title: Biometric AI Compliance
biometrics.opening: Biometric AI agents are strictly regulated. Remote biometric identification in public spaces is PROHIBITED (Art. 5). All other biometric systems require third-party assessment. Compliance is required by August 2, 2026.

faq.q: Is remote biometric identification allowed in the EU?
faq.a: Generally prohibited under Art. 5, with very narrow exceptions for law enforcement.

faq.q: Does emotion recognition AI need compliance?
faq.a: Yes, if used in education or workplace contexts, it is high-risk under Annex III.

critical-infrastructure.title: Critical Infrastructure
critical-infrastructure.opening: AI managing water, energy, or traffic infrastructure are high-risk. Overlaps with NIS2 requirements. High safety priority: every physical action must be HITL-gated.

faq.q: How does this overlap with NIS2?
faq.a: AI compliance must be integrated with NIS2 incident reporting and risk management.

education.title: Education & Training
education.opening: AI for admissions, assessments, or proctoring is high-risk. Must ensure no autonomous denial of access to education.

faq.a: Yes, but results that impact access to education must have human oversight.

hr-employment.title: HR & Employment
hr-employment.opening: AI for CV screening, hiring, or performance monitoring is high-risk. Triggers GDPR DPIA. No autonomous hire/fire decisions.

faq.q: Can AI make final hiring decisions?
faq.a: No. EU law requires a human decision-maker for consequential employment actions.

faq.q: Do I need a GDPR DPIA for HR AI?
faq.a: Almost certainly. SupraWall automates the technical data for the assessment.

healthcare.title: Healthcare & Essential Services
healthcare.opening: Spans medical devices (Aug 2027) and public services (Aug 2026). No autonomous denial of benefits or triage.

faq.q: What is the medical device deadline?
faq.a: AI embedded in medical devices has an extension until August 2027.

law-enforcement.title: Law Enforcement
law-enforcement.opening: Restricted systems for risk scoring or evidence reliability. Third-party assessment mandatory. Prohibits profiling-only actions.

faq.q: Is profiling allowed for law enforcement?
faq.a: Strictly limited. Decisions cannot be based solely on automated profiling.

migration-border-control.title: Migration & Border
migration-border-control.opening: Visa/asylum risk assessments. Third-party mandatory. Hard block on autonomous asylum outcomes.

faq.q: Can AI decide on visa applications?
faq.a: No. Immigration officers must review and sign off on all high-impact actions.

justice-democracy.title: Justice & Democracy
justice-democracy.opening: AI for judicial fact-mapping or electoral influence. Third-party mandatory. All output must be research-only.

faq.a: Yes, but only as a non-binding research aid with explicit uncertainty markers.

hero.title: Every Threat.
hero.description: Deterministic security for the agentic future. Explore the six core capabilities that make SupraWall the production standard for autonomous AI agents.

vault.title: Credential Vault
vault.desc: Zero-knowledge secret injection for AI tools. Agents never see raw API keys or passwords — they only use authorized tokens.

budget.title: Budget Limits
budget.desc: Hard caps on agent API spend. Prevent infinite loops and runaway costs with per-agent circuit breakers.

policy.title: Policy Engine
policy.desc: Deterministic ALLOW/BLOCK rules for tool calls. Enforced outside the LLM context to prevent hallucinations and rogue actions.

pii.title: PII Shield
pii.desc: Automatic outbound data scrubbing. Redact Names, Emails, SSNs, and custom patterns from tool payloads automatically.

audit.title: Audit Trail
audit.desc: Immutable, signed logs of every action. One-click PDF evidence reports specifically for EU AI Act compliance.

prompt.title: Prompt Shield
prompt.desc: SDK-level injection protection. Enforce critical safety rules in binary code, where no indirect prompt injection can reach.

dashboard.title: Consolidated Dashboard
dashboard.desc: Manage all six pillars from one central command center. See your total blocked exfiltrations, tokens scrubbed, and budget saved across your entire fleet.

universal.title: Universal Integration
universal.desc: Whether you ship in LangChain, CrewAI, AutoGen, or Vercel AI, SupraWall works the same. One SDK to standardize security across your entire tech stack.

howItWorks.title: SDK-Level
howItWorks.p: SupraWall sits between the LLM's request and the actual tool execution. It is not inside the prompt. It is binary code running on your server.

final.title: Wait for Chaos
final.description: Don't patch your security one tool at a time. Standardize your agentic architecture with the six pillars of SupraWall.

footer.title: From Learning
footer.description: Don't just read about the future — build it safely. Implement what you've learned with the SupraWall SDK today.

hero.title: Standardize the
hero.description: SupraWall was founded in 2024 with a single conviction: Prompts are not security. As the world moves from Chatbots to Autonomous Agents, the industry needed a deterministic, binary-level safety layer.

philosophy.title: Security,
philosophy.description: The current standard for “AI Safety” is to ask the LLM to be safe. We believe this is fundametally flawed. In a production environment, security must be enforced outside the model's context window — where it can't be manipulated by indirect prompt injections or model drift.

vision.title: Founding Vision
vision.description: To move agentic security into the binary runtime. To provide the world with the 1-line fixa for autonomous chaos. To ensure the agents we build today are the ones we control tomorrow.

items.title: Transparency
items.desc: No magic. We publish our interception methods and encourage third-party security audits.

items.title: Sovereignty
items.desc: Your agents, your rules. We provide the substrate, but you own the enforcement policy.

items.title: Resilience
items.desc: Our SDK is designed for mission-critical tasks in finance, legal, and healthcare.

footer.title: Secure the
footer.description: We are building the trust layer for the agentic internet. Come build it with us.

hero.title: Evidence Delivered.
hero.description: Autonomous agents don't have to be a “black box.” SupraWall creates the immutable record for every tool call and blocked action.

observability.title: Automated
observability.description: The EU AI Act requires developers of “High-Risk” agentic architectures to maintain continuous logging and human oversight. SupraWall automates this with signed, regulator-ready PDF reports produced directly from the SDK.

report.description: This report confirms that of 41,200 agent tool calls, 29 blocks occurred based on Policy DP-14. Human approval was granted by 3 authorized engineers for all sensitive actions.

items.title: Immutable Trail
items.desc: Every action is hashed and signed at the source. Once recorded, the evidence cannot be altered.

items.title: Unified Risk Policy
items.desc: Centralize your security policies across all internal and outbound agents in one dashboard.

items.title: Automated GDPR
items.desc: Instantly satisfy data scrubbing requirements with the PII shield running on every integration.

footer.title: Audit Passed.
footer.description: Don't wait for a compliance investigation. Arm your team with the evidence they need today.

tryIn30Seconds.description: No account required. Auto-detect your framework and wrap your agent with security in one command.

hero.title: Stop Guessing.
hero.description: There are two ways to secure an AI agent: asking it to be safe (Probabilistic) or forcing it to be safe (Deterministic). SupraWall is the latter.

items.title: Lakera
items.desc: Probabilistic prompt-based guardrails vs SDK-level deterministic interception.

items.title: Galileo
items.desc: Monitoring & Observability vs Real-time, SDK-enforced policy boundaries.

items.title: NeMo Guardrails
items.desc: Colang-based logic in the context window vs External binary enforcement.

items.title: Guardrails AI
items.desc: Structured output validation vs Total agentic runtime security.

items.title: Portkey
items.desc: AI Gateways focused on traffic vs Security layers focused on agent actions.

items.title: Straiker
items.desc: Automated agent testing vs Real-time agentic tool interception.

items.q: Is SupraWall just another LLM Guardrail?
items.a: No. Guardrails rely on asking the LLM to behave. SupraWall is an SDK-level interceptor that enforces hard rules regardless of what the LLM wants.

items.q: Can I use SupraWall with LangChain?
items.a: Yes. SupraWall integrates with one command into LangChain, CrewAI, and every major framework.

items.q: Does it add latency to tool calls?
items.a: Minimal. Policy checks are performed locally on your node, typically adding <10ms to the total tool execution time.

items.q: Is there a free tier for developers?
items.a: Yes. Our Community SDK is free for single-agent deployments and research use.

hero.title: Zero-PII Leakage.
hero.description: Stop sensitive data from leaving your network. SupraWall redacts PII before the tool call ever hits the external API.

explain.title: Deterministic Obfuscation
explain.p: Don't rely on the LLM to 'be careful' with sensitive data. SupraWall sits between your agent and the internet, ensuring PII is stripped deterministically at the binary level.

scrubbing.title: Outbound Scrubbing
scrubbing.desc: SSNs, Emails, and Credit Cards replaced.

logs.title: Clean Audit Logs
logs.desc: Compliance records stored with zero unmasked PII.

desc.art5: The PII Shield automatically redacts Names, SSNs, and Emails from tool call parameters before they leave your network, ensuring compliance with data minimization mandates.
desc.art22: SupraWall's Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) protocol ensures that high-impact agentic actions are reviewed by a human operator, satisfying Article 22's oversight requirements.
desc.art30: The immutable, RSA-signed audit trail serves as the primary record for all agentic data processing activities, directly feeding your ROPA reports.

isLangChainSecure.title: LangChain è sicuro?
isLangChainSecure.answer: LangChain è un framework, non un livello di sicurezza. Sebbene le versioni attuali siano più sicure di quelle iniziali, gli agenti LangChain sono intrinsecamente vulnerabili alla prompt injection e all'esecuzione non autorizzata di tool, a meno che non siano protetti da un guardrail deterministico in fase di runtime. La sicurezza deve essere gestita al limite dell'esecuzione dell'SDK per garantire che le azioni autonome rimangano entro i limiti di policy definiti.
isLangChainSecure.risk1Desc: Gli agenti possono decidere autonomamente di richiamare tool shell o database distruttivi.
isLangChainSecure.risk2Desc: I modelli LLM possono accidentalmente divulgare dati personali (PII) attraverso i parametri dei tool in uscita.
isLangChainSecure.solutionP: Per rendere sicuro LangChain in produzione, implementa **SupraWall**. Funge da shim di sicurezza guidato da callback che intercetta ogni invocazione di tool *prima* dell'esecuzione, fornendo un livello di protezione deterministico che persiste anche se il contesto del prompt dell'agente viene compromesso.
isLangChainSecure.guideLink: Visualizza la guida all'implementazione della sicurezza di LangChain

whatIsPromptInjection.title: Cos'è la Prompt Injection?
whatIsPromptInjection.answer: La prompt injection è una vulnerabilità di sicurezza in cui un malintenzionato fornisce input dannosi che dirottano le istruzioni di un LLM, costringendolo a ignorare il prompt di sistema originale e a eseguire azioni non autorizzate, come l'esfiltrazione di dati, l'accesso a tool non autorizzati o l'aggiramento dei guardrail di sicurezza.
whatIsPromptInjection.directDesc: L'utente digita direttamente "Ignora le istruzioni precedenti e..."
whatIsPromptInjection.indirectDesc: L'agente legge un file esterno o una pagina web contenente comandi dannosi.
whatIsPromptInjection.solutionP: La prompt injection non può essere risolta solo con "prompt migliori". Richiede l'Agent Runtime Security (ARS): un livello di intercettazione deterministico che convalida le chiamate ai tool *al di fuori* del contesto del modello LLM.
whatIsPromptInjection.productDesc: Blocca automaticamente l'esecuzione di tool non autorizzati anche se l'LLM è compromesso.
whatIsPromptInjection.guideLink: Leggi la guida completa alla prevenzione della prompt injection

howToComplyArticle12.title: Articolo 12 dell'IA Act UE
howToComplyArticle12.answer: L'Articolo 12 dell'IA Act dell'UE richiede che i sistemi di IA ad "alto rischio" registrino automaticamente gli eventi (log) durante tutto il loro ciclo di vita. Per gli agenti autonomi, questo si traduce in piste di audit immutabili e deterministiche di ogni invocazione di tool, input di dati e origine dell'istruzione, garantendo tracciabilità e responsabilità per le decisioni dell'IA.
howToComplyArticle12.step1Desc: I log devono essere generati in tempo reale, indipendentemente dal contesto del modello LLM.
howToComplyArticle12.step2Desc: Ogni chiamata a un tool deve essere collegata a una versione specifica della policy e a un identificatore di sessione.
howToComplyArticle12.step3Desc: I log di audit devono essere archiviati in un ambiente a prova di manomissione (equivalente a SOC2 tipo 2).
howToComplyArticle12.solutionP: L'SDK di SupraWall funge da motore di conformità per l'Articolo 12 intercettando automaticamente le chiamate ai tool al limite del runtime e trasmettendole a un registro di audit immutabile. Ciò garantisce che ogni azione "ragionata" da un agente sia tracciabile fino a un'istruzione sorgente in caso di audit normativo.

hero.title: I tuoi recruiter usano ChatGPT per i CV.
hero.emphasis: Ecco cosa richiede ora la legge UE.
hero.description: La maggior parte dei team HR in Europa usa già l'IA per lo screening. Quasi nessuno sa che farlo senza protezioni viola la legge dal 2 agosto 2026.

story.label: Una scena comune oggi in azienda
story.quote: Un recruiter apre ChatGPT, incolla un CV e chiede: 'Questa persona è adatta?'. Il LLM risponde. Nessuna traccia di audit. Nessun controllo bias. Nessuna validazione umana. Dal 2 agosto 2026 questa è una violazione punibile fino a 35M€ o il 7% del fatturato mondiale.

items.a: Il board necessita del report con log immutabili, non promesse.
7% Globale sulle vendite, impatto azionario drastico.
La responsabilità cade sull'azienda impiegante, non si può delegare il torto al LLM provider.
Si unisce al RGPD europeo art. 22.
Non serve vietare l'IA, serve il guardrail al Gateway come SupraWall per filtrare ed esigere validazione umana dove richiesto.

items.risk: LLMs usati per NDA e brevetti non chiusi.

actionPlan.title: Il piano del CEO/CTO in 30 Giorni
actionPlan.subtitle: Per non trovarsi spiazzati.

steps.title: Fare Inventario

steps.desc: Individuare se tocchiamo punti di 'Alto Rischio'.

steps.desc: Con il DPO aziendale.

steps.desc: Scrivere 3 code lines di SupraWall proxy (Inibisce dati illegali).

steps.title: Attiva gli Audit Log
steps.desc: Crea il report in PDF finale del Mese chiuso.

steps.title: Relazione Dirigenza
steps.desc: Portare Audit al Consiglio d'Amministrazione (CdA).
